properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 5:43PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
At 542 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm near
Glenwood Springs, moving southwest at 15 mph.

HAZARD...Half inch hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Minor hail damage to vegetation is possible.

Locations impacted include...
Glenwood Springs and Cattle Creek.

This includes the following highways...
Interstate 70 in Colorado between mile markers 111 and 122.
Colorado 82 between mile markers 1 and 10.
properties.areaDesc: Central Colorado River Basin; Gore and Elk Mountains/Central Mountain Valleys; Flat Tops

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 20 at 5:30PM MDT until July 20 at 8:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
East Central San Miguel County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 830 PM MDT.

* At 530 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.5 and 1 inch of rain
has fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Mountain Village.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 5:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
At 530 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm near
Sawpit, or 40 miles south of Montrose, moving west at 15 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph and nickel size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation and
outdoor objects is possible.

This storm will remain over mainly rural areas of southeastern San
Miguel County.

This includes Colorado 145 between mile markers 66 and 69, near mile
marker 78, near mile marker 82, and between mile markers 87 and 89.
properties.areaDesc: Northwestern San Juan Mountains; Southwest San Juan Mountains; Paradox Valley/Lower Dolores River

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 20 at 5:06PM MDT until July 20 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of northwest Colorado, including the following
county, Eagle.

* WHEN...Until 700 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Ponding of water in urban or other areas is occurring or is
imminent.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 506 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. This will cause urban and small stream
flooding. Between 0.5 and 0.7 inches of rain have fallen.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Gypsum, Sweetwater and Dotsero.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 20 at 4:34PM MDT until July 20 at 6:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of northwest Colorado, including the following
county, Eagle.

* WHEN...Until 630 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Water over roadways.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 434 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. This will cause urban and small stream
flooding. Between 0.5 and 0.7 inches of rain have fallen.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Eagle, Gypsum, Avon, Edwards, Wolcott and Eagle-Vail.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 20 at 4:25PM MDT until July 20 at 7:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Small stream flooding caused by excessive rainfall is
expected.

* WHERE...Northwestern Clear Creek County in central Colorado...
Central Summit County in central Colorado...
South Central Grand County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 730 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 425 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. This may cause small
stream flooding. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Silverthorne, Berthoud Falls and Berthoud Pass.
properties.areaDesc: Clear Creek, CO; Grand, CO; Summit, CO
